decisions.title: Sift Edge Sync Architecture: Push vs Pull Model
decisions.context: ENG-8855 prototyping sync, ENG-9558 adding test coverage, ENG-9559/9560/9561 adding metrics/observability. Need to decide on final architecture before GA. Impacts Build the Moat pillar (edge differentiation).

arguments.for: Push model reduces latency, enables real-time decisions at edge, aligns with 'Decision' in Signal>Evidence>Decision
arguments.against: Pull model simpler to implement, easier backpressure handling, lower edge resource requirements

decisions.title: Consolidate Rules/Stats Feature Flags Before Explore V2 Launch
decisions.context: ENG-9557 flagged consolidation needed. Multiple overlapping flags causing confusion. Explore V2 is Nail the Core priority. Launch target Q2 2026.

arguments.for: Reduces technical debt, simplifies rollout logic, prevents configuration errors in production
arguments.against: Requires coordination across teams, may delay other feature work temporarily

decisions.title: Family Data Beta Exit Criteria
decisions.context: ENG-9556 adding beta label, FD-100/101/102 establishing patterns. Need clear path to GA. Embed Deeper pillar relies on multi-run analysis.
decisions.blockedBy: Exception handling patterns (FD-102) and performance benchmarks incomplete

arguments.for: Clear criteria accelerates GA decision, aligns team on quality bar, increases Decisions Captured metric
arguments.against: Premature criteria may force compromises, market may need more beta feedback first

decisions.title: Global Nav Redesign: Proceed to Implementation
decisions.context: ENG-9126 prototypes ready for testing. Impacts all products (Explore V2, Canvas, Rules). Nail the Core requires unified UX.

arguments.for: Prototypes show 40% reduction in nav clicks, unifies cross-product experience, improves decision velocity
arguments.against: High implementation cost across all surfaces, risk of disrupting existing user workflows

decisions.title: Catalog Update Service Schema Finalization
decisions.context: ENG-9425 schema design in progress. Affects Python Rules and Ask SIFT data freshness. Build the Moat through better metadata.

arguments.for: Kafka-based updates enable real-time metadata, improves Ask SIFT response accuracy, scales to 10x catalog size
arguments.against: Complex schema evolution story, requires backward compatibility guarantees, increases operational burden

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy meeting load (80% of work hours). Edge sync architecture discussion consumed 4hrs across 3 meetings. Recommend consolidating Edge working group to single 90min weekly session.
alerts: Canvas PM has 28 meetings this week (recommend delegation)
